LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VOLVO CARS OF NORTH AMERICA, L.L.C. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FILE AN UNDERTAKING TO SECURE AN AWARE FOR COSTS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1030. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. BY NOT ATTACHING THE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORIES, THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE IF DEFENDANT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL. FURTHERMORE, C.C.P. SECTION 1030 IS FOR THE BENEFIT OF CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS A RESIDENT OF CALIFORNIA. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED THE FINANCIAL INABLILITY TO SECURE A BOND. UNDER C.C.P SECTION 995.240, THE COURT WOULD EXCUSE THE POSTING OF A BOND IF THE REQUISITE SHOWING HAD BEEN MADE. COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S ORAL MOTION FOR A CONTINUANCE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A TRIER OF FACT COULD CONCLUDE THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS FROM PRODUCTS MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT DECEDENT HAD CONTACT WITH DEFENDANT'S VEHICLES, BOTH NEW AND USED, AND DEFENDANT'S REPLACEMENT PARTS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT AT TIME OF THE CONTACT THE PRODUCTS CONTAINED ASBESTOS, THAT SUCH ASBESTOS WAS RELEASED AROUND DECEDENT IN ANY OR SUFFICIENT QUANTITIES TO CAUSE THE DISEASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR# 7433

Off Calendar Jul-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Matthew Hickey of Wilson Elser (433-0990) for defendant and moving party
Freda King (208-255-7915) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on factually devoid discovery. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence from which a trier of fact could conclude that decedent was exposed to asbestos from products manufactured or supplied by defendant. Plaintiff has presented evidence that decedent had contact with defendant's vehicles, both new and used, and defendant's replacement parts. Plaintiff has not shown that at time of the contact the products contained asbestos, that such asbestos was released around decedent in any or sufficient quantities to cause the disease. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PJM) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Peppina Thompson, CSR# 7433

MOTION For An Order Requiring Pltf To File An Undertaking To Secure An Aware For Costs Pursuant To Ccp Section 1030
Off Calendar Apr-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VOLVO CARS OF NORTH AMERICA, L.L.C. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FILE AN UNDERTAKING TO SECURE AN AWARE FOR COSTS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1030.
APPEARANCES: FREDA KING, PRO PER (208.255.7915) APPEARING THROUGH COURTCALL ON HER OWN BEHALF. RICHARD R. AMES, ESQUIRE (CARROLL BURDICK & MCDONOUGH, 415.989.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. BY NOT ATTACHING THE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORIES, THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE IF DEFENDANT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL. FURTHERMORE, C.C.P. SECTION 1030 IS FOR THE BENEFIT OF CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS A RESIDENT OF CALIFORNIA. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED THE FINANCIAL INABLILITY TO SECURE A BOND. UNDER C.C.P SECTION 995.240, THE COURT WOULD EXCUSE THE POSTING OF A BOND IF THE REQUISITE SHOWING HAD BEEN MADE. COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S ORAL MOTION FOR A CONTINUANCE. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
